channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Frenchman convicted in missing body case confesses to killing his wife, lawyer says
item.description: Cédric Jubillar, a painter and plasterer who was sentenced last October for killing his wife Delphine, has finally confessed to the murder from his prison cell, his lawyer said Monday. The 38-year-old said he was ready to tell investigators the location of his wife's body, which was never found.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Death toll from DR Congo Ebola outbreak passes 500 as healthcare workers threaten strike
item.description: The deadly Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has killed more than 500 people, the UN health agency's figures showed Monday. Healthcare workers in Ituri province, the heart of the outbreak, on Sunday issued a 24-hour notice threatening to strike if the government did not improve their working conditions and pay them promised benefits.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Tadej Pogacar wins third stage of Tour de France to take yellow jersey from Vingegaard
item.description: Slovenian cyclist Tadej Pogacar won stage three of the Tour de France Monday, taking the yellow jersey from his rival Jonas Vingegaard.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Boston: The cradle of the American Revolution
item.description: Americans celebrated their independence on July 4th. But did you know that the revolution that led to the creation of the US began in Boston? We take you on a tour of this revolutionary city.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ukraine must 'get the support it needs': allies prepare for NATO summit
item.description: "Allies must ensure that Ukraine receives the support it needs in its war with Russia" according to NATO chief Mark Rutte, ahead of a summit set to take place in Ankara, Turkey. He has also said allies will be announcing contracts worth tens of billions to "deter and defend". FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us what to expect from the summit, and what it means for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump confirms call at centre of red card controversy 'with massive political consequences'
item.description: President Donald Trump confirmed he asked FIFA boss Gianni Infantino to review the "horrible" decision to hand a red card to star US striker Folarin Balogun but said he did not request it be overturned. Belgium, who the US will be playing in the quarterfinal, have called the decision 'incomprehensible' and a 'a blatant violation of the most basic rules of football and sport'. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes tells us more and whether this could change Belgium's chance of winning.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Algeria's ruling FLN secures most seats in parliament with historically low turnout
item.description: Algeria's ruling National Liberation Front won the most seats in the country's parliamentary elections, the election board said Monday, securing 90 out of 407 in the July 2 vote. But the poll was marred by record-low turnout after the government barred more than a third of prospective candidates from running.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France at 'turning point' on how to prevent and deal with sexual violence
item.description: In a new twist in a femicide case that has gripped France for years, Cédric Jubillar has admitted to the murder of his wife and has told authorities he would help them find her body. Delphine Jubillar had disappeared in late December 2020 as she was asking for divorce and had started a new relationship. FRANCE 24 Carys Garland tells us more about the case, and the growing tension in France over the killing of women and children.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'His resilience, his words, his character': Mourners grieve late supreme leader Khamenei
item.description: A sea of mourners filled the streets of Tehran on Monday for the funeral procession of late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. FRANCE 24 spoke to some of those who had travelled across the country to pay their respects for the man who led the Islamic Republic for almost four decades until he was killed in US-Israeli air strikes.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Avignon turns 80: Wagner Moura, Isabelle Huppert attend France's oldest theatre festival
item.description: FRANCE 24's Olivia Salazar-Winspear brings us all the latest from the Avignon performing arts festival, which is celebrating its 80th anniversary. This year, Golden Globe winners, Brazilian actor Wagner Moura and French actress Isabelle Huppert are starring in shows. The festival has also chosen Korean as its language of honour and turns the spotlight on artists from the Mediterranean region. Salazar-Winspear also talks to the co-director of Avignon's fringe program. It's known as the "off" in French and features more than 1,700 shows performed in public spaces around the city. Finally, she gives us her recommendation for a fringe show that is not to be missed.
item.dc:creator: Olivia SALAZAR-WINSPEAR

item.title: Earthquake damage threatens Venezuela's beach tourism centers
item.description: The earthquakes that hit Venezuela in late June have devastated seaside towns where tourism has been a vital economic lifeline for an already struggling population. Also, tanker traffic picks up through the Strait of Hormuz, and Europe seeks to convince the US that it's boosting defence spending.
item.dc:creator: Bryan QUINN

item.title: Does the French right have a chance of defeating the far right in the election?
item.description: Right-wing Edouard Philippe has officially launched his presidential campaign less than a year ahead of the 2027 election in France. Philippe was the first one to say explicitly he would run for France's highest office. Now, polls give the ex-PM the best chance to defeat the far right. FRANCE 24's Marc Perelman tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iranians struggle as ongoing economic crisis shows no sign of abatting
item.description: Across Iran, families are grappling with soaring living costs, as the combined weight of war, subsidy reforms and sanctions pushes basic necessities beyond the reach of many. Some Iranians had hoped foreign intervention would unseat the regime - instead, the US-Israel war has crushed livelihoods.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: In pictures: Marine Le Pen’s political rise and frustrated presidential ambitions
item.description: French far-right figurehead Marine Le Pen could see her 40-year political career come to an end on Tuesday if French courts rule that she is guilty of embezzling public funds and ineligible to stand for the presidency in 2027. FRANCE 24 takes a look at how she rose to prominence in France and brought her National Rally party into mainstream politics.
item.dc:creator: Romain BRUNET

item.title: How are the millions attending Khamenei's funeral remembering the late ayatollah?
item.description: The funeral procession for Iran's late supreme leader Ali Khamenei has begun in Tehran. Millions are expected to attend, possibly leading to crowds that could rival those that turned out for his predecessor nearly four decades ago. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah went to the procession and tells us why so many admired the late ayatollah.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Greece's Thessaloniki threatened by spreading wildfire
item.description: A fast-moving wildfire broke out in Greece's second largest city on July 4, forcing residents of three small suburbs north of the city to evacuate. The fire ignited a ravine in a mountainous area and spread quickly, with strong winds hampering efforts to contain the fire. According to the country's public broadcaster, a 76-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of starting the wildfire, as he allegedly ignited the blaze after his vehicle produced sparks.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Dissolution of Hamas's Gaza governing body won't bring 'significant change of life'
item.description: Hamas announced on July 6 the dissolution of the body that has governed the Gaza Strip for nearly two decades. The decision would clear the way for a technocratic committee to implement civilian rule. But questions remain. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more from Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: England hold on against Mexico while Norway oust Brazil
item.description: The Three Lions defied high altitude and a roaring home crowd to beat Mexico 3-2. Man of the match Jude Bellingham scored twice in the space of two minutes while Harry Kane's penalty kept England ahead of a fierce Mexico team. Meanwhile five-time champions Brazil were knocked-out by Norway, as Erling Haaland did what he does best to seal the win 2-1.
item.dc:creator: James VASINA

item.title: As a new heatwave looms, Europe falls for Chinese air conditioners
item.description: Hundreds of people besieged supermarkets in and around Paris, with scuffles and shouting matches breaking out as residents scrambled to get their hands on bargain air-cooling units before the next heatwave. With the hunt for affordability, Europeans are turning to Chinese AC units - despite trade tensions between Beijing and Brussels. FRANCE 24's Yena Lee tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, clearing way for technocratic committee
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Genuine admiration and emotion' in Tehran's streets as Iran mourns supreme leader
item.description: Vast crowds have gathered for the funeral procession of Iran's slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei in Tehran on July 6, with authorities estimating millions were on the streets. The final public tribute is expected to last around 10 hours. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah tells us more on what to know.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Venezuelans mobilise to deal with earthquakes' consequences: FRANCE 24 reports
item.description: As more than ten days have passed since Venezuela was struck by twin earthquakes, the death toll remains uncertain, with an official balance of at least 3,342 people killed. On the northern coastal part of the country, the hardest-hit region, survivors and volunteers from across the country are taking matters into their own hands : treating the injured, finding shelter, searching for the missing. FRANCE 24's Pascale Mariani and Maxime Pluvinet spoke to residents
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, clearing way for technocratic committee
item.description: The Palestinian movement Hamas announced Monday that it had dissolved its governing body in the Gaza Strip after nearly 20 years in power, paving the way for a technocratic committee to administer the territory.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
